title: Add Set Items
content:
### Adding Items to a Set

---

### `.add()` -- Add Single Item

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
fruits.add("orange")
print(fruits)  # {"apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange"}

# Adding a duplicate has no effect
fruits.add("apple")
print(fruits)  # Unchanged
```

---

### `.update()` -- Add Multiple Items

```python
fruits = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
fruits.update(["orange", "kiwi", "grape"])
print(fruits)
```

`.update()` accepts any iterable (list, tuple, set, etc.).

---

### Union Operator `|`

```python
set1 = {1, 2, 3}
set2 = {3, 4, 5}
result = set1 | set2
print(result)  # {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
```

Creates a **new** set with elements from both.

---

### In-Place Update

```python
set1 = {1, 2, 3}
set2 = {3, 4, 5}
set1 |= set2  # Same as set1.update(set2)
print(set1)  # {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
```

---

> **Difference:** `.add()` takes a single element. `.update()` takes an iterable and adds each element. `|` creates a new set.

quiz.question: Which method adds a single element to a set?
